content: We can reframe LoCoMo motion planning latency optimization as an intrinsic curiosity problem for autonomous mobile agents, where the ICM curiosity mechanism rewards the agent for exploring low-latency motion planning trajectories that the system has not tested frequently, rather than only converging on a precomputed feasible path. This creates a self-optimizing loop where the agent continuously discovers faster, more adaptive motion plans for novel dynamic environments without explicit external reward tuning.
